title: Domain Records, Pure Pipeline Stages, and Serialization · Behavior Case: Domain Records
summary: The program passes nested dictionaries with undocumented keys, mutates statistics during formatting, serializes Python repr output, and cannot isolate which stage changed a result. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the domain records increment in typed cumulative analysis pipeline. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.
Compare batch and streaming results across adversarial split points, malformed data, and final fragments.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-domain-records — Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-domain-model-pipeline-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-domain-model-pipeline-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_domain_model_pipeline_5(stages: int = 7, immutable_records: bool = True, typed_boundaries: bool = True, schema_version: int = 1, stage_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if stages < 5 or schema_version < 1:
        return False, stages, "pipeline-shape"
    if not immutable_records:
        return False, stages, "mutable-evidence"
    if not typed_boundaries:
        return False, stages, "untyped-stage"
    if stage_failures > 0:
        return False, stage_failures, "stage-failure"
    assert stages >= 5
    return True, schema_version, "versioned-pipeline"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Domain Records model · Theory Domain Records, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Domain Records precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Domain Records model · Theory Domain Records, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 9280 during a multilingual community archive intake. Build deterministic Python evidence to compose typed stages and immutable evidence under a versioned serialization schema; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Model input identity, policy, token, statistics, ranking row, and report metadata with explicit dataclasses or typed records.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Domain Records model · Theory Domain Records, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A dictionary is self-documenting because its keys are visible at runtime.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-domain-records-1b4pzd(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_domain_records_1b4pzd\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stages < 5)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not immutable_records)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not typed_boundaries)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stage_failures > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
